name: Blender Ketchup
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the tomatoes and dip them in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds, or until the skins split. Then dip them in cold water and slip off the skins.
2. Core and quarter the tomatoes, removing any seeds.
3. Remove the skins from the peppers and slice them into strips. Peel the onions and quarter them.
4. Blend the tomatoes, peppers, and onions at high speed for 5 seconds using an electric blender.
5. Pour the mixture into a 3- to 4-gallon stock pot or large kettle.
6. Heat gently and boil for 60 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Add vinegar, sugar, salt, and a spice bag containing dry mustard, ground red pepper, whole allspice, and whole cloves.
8. Continue boiling and stirring until the volume is reduced by half and the ketchup rounds up on a spoon with no separation of liquid and solids.
9. Remove the spice bag and fill jars, leaving 1/8-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and follow process times for regular ketchup according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Blender Ketchup in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 min.
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 min.
Above 6,000 ft: 25 min.
